title: Sunrun's Virtual Power Plant Programs Complete Successful 2024
text: Sunrun announced successful expansion of virtual power plant (VPP) portfolio in 2024, with programs across nine states and territories supporting grid operations at combined peak of nearly 80 megawatts. More than 20,000 Sunrun customers engaged in 16 virtual power plant initiatives throughout 2024, utilizing residential solar and battery systems to provide stored energy during critical grid events. California's CalReady program enrolled over 16,000 customers, delivering average of 48 megawatts during peak summer hours with maximum output reaching 54 megawatts. Puerto Rico's PowerOn Puerto Rico included over 4,000 customers' battery systems providing emergency support during 70+ energy shortfall events. Texas launched new partnerships with Tesla Electric and Vistra offering VPP programs with customer payments and credits for battery participation. New York's largest residential VPP launched with Orange & Rockland Utilities providing discounted batteries to 300+ participating customers. Maryland launched America's first vehicle-to-home VPP with Ford F-150 Lightning owners partnering with Baltimore Gas and Electric. CEO Mary Powell noted that utilities are at point where they cannot grow fast enough for increased demand, positioning Sunrun's programs as critical grid infrastructure solutions. The programs provide customers annual payments while Sunrun earns recurring revenue through program participation.
